foldx_ddg_gearbind.description: Run FoldX/DDG/GearBind analysis on AlphaFold3 PDB files, output antibody candidates and result CSV

input_params.name: input_file_path
input_params.description: The path to the AlphaFold3 PDB file that will be analyzed for antibody candidates and stability using FoldX/DDG/GearBind.

metabcr.description: MetaBCR deep learning model predicts antibody-antigen binding affinity, stream progress

input_params.name: antibody_file
input_params.description: A CSV file containing the sequences and other relevant information of the antibodies to be analyzed for binding affinity.

input_params.name: antigen_file
input_params.description: A CSV file containing the antigen sequences to be analyzed for binding affinity with antibodies.

input_params.name: antigen_name
input_params.description: The name of the antigen for which the antibody-antigen binding affinity will be predicted. Must be one of the following: flu, sars, rsv, hiv.

input_params.name: output_file_path
input_params.description: The file path where the output of the binding affinity predictions will be saved.

search_airr_repertoires.description: Search BCR repertoires in AIRR library, return matching studies and sample information

input_params.name: disease
input_params.description: The disease associated with the BCR repertoires to search for. This parameter allows filtering of studies and samples based on a specific disease condition.

input_params.name: tissue
input_params.description: The type of tissue from which the BCR repertoire was sampled, e.g., 'blood', 'bone marrow', or 'spleen'.

input_params.name: species
input_params.description: The species from which the BCR repertoires are derived. Options are limited to human, mouse, rat, or rabbit.

input_params.name: cell_subset
input_params.description: A string specifying the subset of cells to be included in the search, such as 'B cells', 'T cells', or any other specific cell type.

input_params.name: repository
input_params.description: The repository to search for BCR repertoires, limited to the following options: all, vdjserver, ireceptor, covid19.

input_params.name: max_results
input_params.description: The maximum number of search results to return. This parameter allows you to limit the output to a specified number of top matches.

download_airr_sequences.description: Download BCR sequences from AIRR library, output AIRR format files compatible with IgBLAST

input_params.name: repertoire_id
input_params.description: The unique identifier for the repertoire from which BCR sequences will be downloaded.

input_params.name: filters
input_params.description: A dictionary specifying the filters to be applied on the BCR sequences, such as 'v_call', 'j_call', or 'sequence_length'. Each key is a filter name and each value is the corresponding filter value or list of values.

input_params.name: format
input_params.description: The output format for the downloaded BCR sequences. Must be one of the following: airr, json.

input_params.name: max_sequences
input_params.description: The maximum number of BCR sequences to download from the AIRR library. There is no upper limit, and any positive integer can be specified.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the downloaded AIRR format files will be saved. If not provided, the current working directory is used.

get_airr_study_metadata.description: Get detailed metadata for AIRR studies including samples and disease information

input_params.name: study_id
input_params.description: A unique identifier for the AIRR study to retrieve metadata for.

input_params.name: repository
input_params.description: Specifies the repository from which to retrieve the AIRR study metadata. Options are limited to 'auto', 'vdjserver', 'ireceptor', and 'covid19'.

filter_by_vdj_genes.description: Filter sequences by V(D)J gene usage patterns, output filtered results and gene usage statistics

input_params.name: repertoire_id
input_params.description: A unique identifier for the repertoire dataset to be filtered.

input_params.name: v_gene
input_params.description: The V gene to filter the sequences by. If provided, only sequences with this V gene will be included in the output.

input_params.name: d_gene
input_params.description: The D gene to filter the sequences by. If provided, only sequences with this specific D gene will be included in the output.

input_params.name: j_gene
input_params.description: The J gene to filter the sequences by. This parameter allows specifying a particular J gene to include in the output.

input_params.name: combination_logic
input_params.description: The logical operator to use when applying multiple gene usage filters. Options are 'AND' for requiring all specified genes, or 'OR' for any of the specified genes.

get_airr_statistics.description: Get BCR repertoire statistics including sequence count, V gene usage, CDR3 length distribution

input_params.name: repertoire_id
input_params.description: A unique identifier for the BCR repertoire dataset to analyze.

input_params.name: metrics
input_params.description: The specific metric to calculate for the BCR repertoire, must be one of: diversity, clonality, v_usage, cdr3_length, mutation_frequency.

input_params.name: input_file_path
input_params.description: The file path to the input data in xlsx or csv format, which contains the necessary information for the alphafold3 service to process.

input_params.name: antigen_name
input_params.description: The name of the antigen to be analyzed. This is an optional parameter that can be used to label or identify the specific antigen in the output.

input_params.name: gpu_device
input_params.description: The identifier of the GPU device to be used for computation. If not provided, the default GPU will be used.

number_antibody_batch.description: Batch number antibody sequences using ANARCI, output complete numbering results

input_params.name: sequences
input_params.description: A list of dictionaries, each containing a single antibody sequence to be numbered. Each dictionary should have a key 'sequence' with the corresponding value being the amino acid sequence as a string.

input_params.name: scheme
input_params.description: The numbering scheme to be used for the antibody sequences. Options are constrained to chothia, kabat, imgt, martin, aho, or wolfguy.

input_params.name: assign_germline
input_params.description: A boolean flag to indicate whether to assign the germline sequence during the numbering process. If true, the germline will be assigned; if false, it will not.

number_single_sequence.description: Quickly number a single antibody sequence using ANARCI, output numbering and chain type

input_params.name: sequence
input_params.description: The amino acid sequence of a single antibody chain to be numbered. The sequence should be provided as a string of single-letter amino acid codes.

input_params.name: scheme
input_params.description: The numbering scheme to be used for the antibody sequence. Options are constrained to chothia, kabat, imgt, martin, aho, or wolfguy.

search_geo_studies.description: Search BCR-seq studies in GEO using NCBI E-utilities, return matching results

input_params.name: keywords
input_params.description: A string containing specific terms or phrases to search for in BCR-seq studies within the GEO database.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The organism to filter BCR-seq studies by. Must be one of the following: Homo sapiens, Mus musculus, Rattus norvegicus.

input_params.name: study_type
input_params.description: The type of study to search for, such as 'BCR-seq' or other specific study types.

input_params.name: max_results
input_params.description: The maximum number of results to return from the search. This is an integer value and there are no specific options; any positive integer can be used.

get_geo_metadata.description: Get detailed metadata for GEO studies/samples

input_params.name: geo_id
input_params.description: The unique identifier for a GEO study or sample, typically in the format GSMXXXXX for samples or GSEXXXXX for studies.

input_params.name: include_samples
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to include sample-level metadata in the response. If true, the response will contain detailed information for each sample; if false, only study-level metadata will be provided.

download_geo_sequences.description: Download BCR-seq data from GEO, support multiple output formats, return FTP download links

input_params.name: geo_id
input_params.description: The GEO accession ID for the BCR-seq dataset to be downloaded. This is a unique identifier assigned by the Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO) database.

input_params.name: data_format
input_params.description: Specifies the format of the BCR-seq data to download. Options are 'raw', 'processed', or 'supplementary'.

input_params.name: output_format
input_params.description: The desired format for the output data. Must be one of: fasta, fastq, tsv, json, airr.

input_params.name: max_sequences
input_params.description: The maximum number of sequences to download. This parameter allows the user to limit the dataset size, which is useful for testing or when full data is not required.

list_geo_bcr_datasets.description: List filtered high-quality BCR-seq datasets in GEO

input_params.name: curated_only
input_params.description: A boolean flag to indicate whether to return only datasets that have been curated for high quality. If true, only curated datasets are listed; if false, all datasets are included.

get_all_geo_bcr_studies.description: Retrieve all BCR-seq studies from GEO, return deduplicated metadata, support species filtering

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The species to filter the BCR-seq studies by. Options are limited to 'Homo sapiens', 'Mus musculus', or 'all' for no filtering.

input_params.name: max_results
input_params.description: The maximum number of results to return. This parameter allows the user to limit the output size, useful for testing or when only a subset of data is needed.

input_params.name: include_metadata
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to include detailed metadata for each study in the results.

search_iedb_epitopes.description: Search antibody-epitope binding data in IEDB, support antigen/disease filtering

input_params.name: antigen
input_params.description: The specific antigen to filter the search results. If provided, only epitopes associated with this antigen will be returned.

input_params.name: antibody_sequence
input_params.description: The amino acid sequence of the antibody for which to search binding data. This parameter is optional and can be left empty if not provided.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The organism from which the epitope is derived. Options are limited to Homo sapiens or Mus musculus.

input_params.name: disease
input_params.description: The name of the disease associated with the epitope. This parameter is optional and can be used to filter results by a specific disease.

input_params.name: assay_type
input_params.description: The type of assay used to measure the interaction, constrained to one of the following options: binding, neutralization, or ELISA.

input_params.name: max_results
input_params.description: The maximum number of results to return from the search. This is an integer value with no specific options, allowing the user to set any positive integer as the limit.

get_iedb_antibody_data.description: Get detailed antibody information from IEDB including sequences, epitopes, and PDB structures

input_params.name: antibody_id
input_params.description: A unique identifier for the antibody record in the IEDB database. This parameter is optional and can be used to fetch specific antibody data.

input_params.name: antibody_name
input_params.description: The name of the antibody for which detailed information is to be retrieved. This can include any specific identifier or common name used for the antibody.

input_params.name: include_structures
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to include PDB structures in the antibody data retrieved from IEDB. If true, the response will contain PDB structure information; if false, it will not.

validate_epitope_binding.description: Validate CDR3 sequence binding to known epitopes, output matching results and similarity scores

input_params.name: cdr3_sequences
input_params.description: A list of CDR3 sequences to be validated for binding to known epitopes. Each sequence should be a string of amino acid residues.

input_params.name: epitope_sequence
input_params.description: The amino acid sequence of the epitope to be validated for binding. This should be a string of single-letter amino acid codes.

input_params.name: antigen
input_params.description: The antigen sequence to which the CDR3 sequence is being tested for binding. This should be a string representing the amino acid sequence of the antigen.

download_iedb_dataset.description: Batch download IEDB epitope data, support disease/antigen filtering, output JSON/TSV

input_params.name: disease
input_params.description: The name of the disease to filter the IEDB epitope data. If not provided, no disease filter is applied.

input_params.name: antigen
input_params.description: The specific antigen to filter the IEDB epitope data. If provided, only epitopes related to this antigen will be included in the download.

input_params.name: assay_type
input_params.description: The type of assay to filter the IEDB epitope data by. Options are constrained to 'all', 'binding', or 'neutralization'.

input_params.name: format
input_params.description: The format in which the IEDB epitope data will be downloaded. Options are limited to 'json' for JSON format or 'tsv' for tab-separated values.

input_params.name: max_records
input_params.description: The maximum number of records to be downloaded from the IEDB dataset. There are no specific options; any positive integer is acceptable.

enrich_sequences_with_iedb.description: Enrich GEO sequences with IEDB epitope data, add epitope matching and structural information

input_params.name: sequences
input_params.description: A list of dictionaries, each containing sequence information such as the sequence ID and the amino acid sequence to be enriched with IEDB epitope data.

input_params.name: iedb_search
input_params.description: A boolean flag to indicate whether to search for and include IEDB epitope data in the sequence enrichment process.

input_params.name: sabdab_search
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to perform a SAbDab search for antibody-antigen structures. If true, the tool will search for and include relevant structural information from the SAbDab database.

map_cdr3_to_epitopes.description: Batch map CDR3 sequences to known epitopes, output binding predictions and confidence

input_params.name: cdr3_list
input_params.description: A list of CDR3 amino acid sequences to be mapped to known epitopes for binding predictions.

input_params.name: disease_context
input_params.description: The disease context in which the CDR3 sequences and epitopes are being analyzed. This can be any string that specifies the disease or condition, such as 'cancer', 'autoimmune', etc.

input_params.name: confidence_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum confidence level required for a CDR3-epitope binding prediction to be included in the results. This value must be between 0 and 1, where 1 indicates the highest confidence.

analyze_vdj_batch.description: Batch analyze V(D)J recombination using IgBLAST+ChangeO, output AIRR format results

input_params.name: sequences
input_params.description: A FASTA file containing the nucleotide sequences to be analyzed for V(D)J recombination.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The species of the input sequences, must be one of: human, mouse, rabbit, rat, rhesus, pig.

input_params.name: receptor_type
input_params.description: Specifies the type of receptor to be analyzed, must be either 'Ig' for immunoglobulin or 'TCR' for T-cell receptor.

input_params.name: locus
input_params.description: The specific immunoglobulin or T-cell receptor locus to be analyzed. Must be one of the following: IGH, IGK, IGL, TRA, TRB, TRG, TRD.

input_params.name: timeout
input_params.description: The maximum time in seconds to wait for the analysis to complete before timing out.

extract_cdr3_from_airr.description: Extract CDR3 information from AIRR data, support multiple input formats, stream progress

input_params.name: airr_results
input_params.description: A file containing AIRR (Adaptive Immune Receptor Repertoire) data in CSV, TSV, or JSON format from which CDR3 information will be extracted.

search_oas_studies.description: Search OAS studies by conditions, return matching study lists and sequence statistics

input_params.name: disease
input_params.description: The specific disease or condition to filter the OAS studies. If provided, only studies related to this disease will be returned.

input_params.name: tissue
input_params.description: The specific tissue type to filter the studies. If not provided, all tissues are considered.

input_params.name: species
input_params.description: The species to filter the OAS studies by. Options are limited to: human, mouse, rabbit, rat, rhesus_monkey, camel.

input_params.name: min_sequences
input_params.description: The minimum number of sequences a study must have to be included in the search results. If not provided, no minimum sequence count is enforced.

input_params.name: paired
input_params.description: Indicates whether the studies should include paired-end sequencing data. True for including only studies with paired-end data, False for excluding them.

download_oas_dataset.description: Download OAS datasets, support resumable downloads, convert to AIRR format, return cache status

input_params.name: study_id
input_params.description: The unique identifier for the study from which to download the dataset.

input_params.name: data_units
input_params.description: A list of specific data units to be downloaded. If not provided, all available data units will be downloaded.

input_params.name: format
input_params.description: The desired output format for the downloaded dataset. Options are limited to airr, fasta, or csv.

input_params.name: max_sequences
input_params.description: The maximum number of sequences to download from the dataset. If not provided, all sequences will be downloaded.

input_params.name: cache
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to use the cache for the dataset. If true, the tool will attempt to retrieve the dataset from the cache; if false, it will download the dataset regardless of cache status.

get_oas_summary.description: Get OAS database or specific study statistics including V gene and isotype distribution

input_params.name: study_id
input_params.description: The unique identifier for a specific study. If not provided, general OAS database statistics will be returned.

filter_oas_by_vgene.description: Filter OAS sequences by V gene, output filtered results and statistics

input_params.name: study_id
input_params.description: The unique identifier for the study from which the sequences are to be filtered.

input_params.name: v_gene
input_params.description: The V gene to filter the OAS sequences by. If not provided, no V gene filtering is applied.

input_params.name: j_gene
input_params.description: The J gene to filter the OAS sequences by. If not provided, no filtering is applied based on J gene.

input_params.name: cdr3_pattern
input_params.description: A string representing a regular expression pattern to filter sequences by CDR3 region. If provided, only sequences with a CDR3 matching this pattern will be included.

input_params.name: min_identity
input_params.description: The minimum identity percentage required for a sequence to be included in the results. This is a floating point number, typically between 0 and 100, representing the percentage of sequence identity.

batch_process_oas.description: Batch download/analyze OAS datasets, output processing results and success/failure statistics

input_params.name: study_ids
input_params.description: A list of unique study identifiers for the OAS datasets to be processed. Each identifier should correspond to a specific study in the OAS database.

input_params.name: operation
input_params.description: Specifies the type of operation to be performed on the OAS datasets. Options are limited to 'download', 'analyze', or 'convert'.

input_params.name: parallel
input_params.description: Indicates whether the processing of OAS datasets should be executed in parallel to potentially speed up the operation.

input_params.name: batch_size
input_params.description: The number of OAS datasets to be processed in each batch. There are no specific options; any positive integer is acceptable.

get_cache_info.description: Get OAS cache information including cached study count and disk usage

get_all_oas_studies.description: Get all OAS study lists including species/disease/tissue classifications, support caching

input_params.name: include_metadata
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to include additional metadata such as species, disease, and tissue classifications in the response.

input_params.name: force_refresh
input_params.description: A boolean flag that, when set to true, forces the tool to bypass the cache and fetch the latest data from the source. If false or not provided, the tool may use cached data if available.

clear_cache.description: Clear OAS cache, free disk space, return cleanup statistics

input_params.name: keep_recent
input_params.description: The number of most recent cache entries to keep, with all others being cleared.

antigen_binding_prediction_visualization.description: Visualize single-cell B cell antigen binding predictions, output binding distribution plots, statistics, and CSV

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing single-cell B cell antigen binding predictions. The file should be an .rds file with the required columns for analysis.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the input data and output files will be stored. This should be an accessible file path.

input_params.name: binding_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum binding affinity value to consider a B cell as having significant antigen binding. This threshold is used to filter the data for visualization and analysis.

bcell_celltype_distribution_analysis.description: Single-cell B cell subtype distribution analysis, output distribution charts, cell proportions, and CSV

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing single-cell B cell data. The file should be an .rds file.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the input files are located and where the output files will be saved.

binding_prediction_interval_distribution.description: Antigen binding prediction value interval distribution analysis, output histograms, CDF curves, and quantile statistics

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing antigen binding prediction values. The file should be an .rds file.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the analysis results and output files will be stored. This should be a valid path accessible to the system running the analysis.

input_params.name: interval_step
input_params.description: The size of each interval step used for binning the binding prediction values in the distribution analysis.

input_params.name: data_min
input_params.description: The minimum value of the data range to be considered for the analysis. This sets the lower bound for the interval distribution.

input_params.name: data_max
input_params.description: The maximum value of the data range to be considered for the analysis. This sets the upper limit for the binding prediction values that will be included in the distribution analysis.

umap_dimensionality_reduction_visualizat.description: Single-cell B cell UMAP dimensionality reduction and visualization, output publication-quality UMAP plots and coordinate data

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: The file path to the input RDS data containing single-cell B cell expression data.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the input data and output files will be stored. This should be a valid path on the file system.

bcell_marker_gene_dotplot_analysis.description: B cell subtype marker gene expression dot plot analysis, output dot plots, expression statistics, and gene lists

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing gene expression data for B cell subtypes.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the analysis results and output files will be stored.

input_params.name: min_pct
input_params.description: The minimum percentage of cells in a cluster that must express the gene for it to be included in the analysis. This value is a float between 0 and 1.

input_params.name: min_expression
input_params.description: The minimum expression level threshold for a gene to be considered expressed in the analysis. This value is used to filter out genes with low expression levels.

antigen_binding_neutralization_density_v.description: UMAP density visualization for single-cell antigen binding/neutralization predictions, output density plots, statistics, and coordinates

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing single-cell antigen binding/neutralization data.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the input data and output files will be stored. This should be a valid path on the file system.

input_params.name: prediction_keywords
input_params.description: A string containing keywords that specify the type of antigen binding or neutralization predictions to be visualized. This can include terms like 'binding', 'neutralization', or specific antigen names.

input_params.name: na_strategy
input_params.description: Strategy for handling missing or not applicable (NA) values in the data. Options are 'exclude_cells' to remove cells with NA values, 'replace_zero' to replace NA values with zero, or 'replace_median' to replace NA values with the median of the available data.

input_params.name: feature_priority
input_params.description: Determines the priority of features in the visualization. Options are 'neutralization_first', 'binding_first', or 'highest_value'.

bcell_celltype_umap_visualization.description: UMAP spatial distribution visualization for single-cell B cell subtypes, output publication-quality UMAP plots and statistics

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing single-cell B cell subtype data.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the input data and output files will be stored. This should be an accessible file path on the system.

input_params.name: celltype_column
input_params.description: The name of the column in the input data that contains the B cell subtype classifications.

bcell_marker_gene_expression_dotplot.description: Visualize B cell subtype marker gene expression, output dot plots, statistics, and PDF

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing B cell subtype marker gene expression data.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the output files will be saved. This should be a valid path on the system.

input_params.name: celltype_column
input_params.description: The name of the column in the input data that specifies the cell type for each entry. This column is used to categorize and visualize B cell subtypes.

differential_gene_correlation_analysis.description: Differential gene correlation analysis, output correlation scatter plots, statistics, and CSV

input_params.name: deg_file1
input_params.description: Path to the first differential gene expression file, typically a CSV or TSV containing gene identifiers and their corresponding expression levels.

input_params.name: deg_file2
input_params.description: Path to the second file containing differentially expressed genes data, typically in a tabular format such as CSV or TSV.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the input files are located and where the output files will be saved.

input_params.name: dataset1_name
input_params.description: The name of the first dataset to be used in the differential gene correlation analysis. This should be a string that uniquely identifies the dataset.

input_params.name: dataset2_name
input_params.description: The name of the second dataset to be used in the differential gene correlation analysis. This should be a string that uniquely identifies the dataset.

input_params.name: p_value_threshold
input_params.description: The threshold for the p-value used to determine statistical significance in the correlation analysis. It should be a float between 0 and 1, where a lower value indicates a more stringent significance level.

input_params.name: min_common_genes
input_params.description: The minimum number of common genes required for two samples to be included in the correlation analysis.

input_params.name: highlight_genes
input_params.description: A comma-separated list of gene names to be highlighted in the correlation scatter plots and analysis output.

differential_gene_expression_correlation.description: Integrated pipeline for differential gene volcano plots and correlation analysis, output complete analysis files

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: The file path to the input RDS data containing gene expression levels for different samples.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The root directory where all the analysis files and outputs will be stored. This should be an accessible path on the system running the pipeline.

input_params.name: logfc_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum log2 fold change value for a gene to be considered significantly differentially expressed. Genes with a log2 fold change greater than this threshold (in absolute value) will be highlighted in the analysis.

input_params.name: min_pct
input_params.description: The minimum percentage of samples that must have a non-zero expression value for a gene to be included in the analysis. This ensures that only genes with sufficient data coverage are considered.

input_params.name: p_value_threshold
input_params.description: The threshold for the p-value used to determine statistical significance in differential gene expression. A lower p-value indicates a more stringent threshold.

input_params.name: min_common_genes
input_params.description: The minimum number of common genes required for a pair of samples to be included in the correlation analysis. This ensures that only sample pairs with a sufficient number of shared genes are analyzed, enhancing the reliability of the correlation results.

input_params.name: highlight_genes
input_params.description: A comma-separated list of gene symbols to be highlighted in the volcano plot and correlation analysis. These genes will be visually emphasized for easier identification.

prediction_value_density_visualization.description: UMAP density visualization of prediction values, output density plots, threshold analysis, and coordinate data

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing prediction values for UMAP density visualization.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the output files, including density plots and coordinate data, will be saved.

input_params.name: prediction_keywords
input_params.description: A string containing keywords or phrases that are used to filter and label the prediction values in the UMAP density visualization.

input_params.name: prediction_threshold
input_params.description: The threshold value used to categorize prediction values for density visualization. This float determines the cutoff point for classifying predictions as significant or not.

pseudotime_trajectory_analysis.description: Analyze B cell pseudotime trajectories using Monocle3, output trajectory plots, CDS objects, and pseudotime data

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: The path to the input RDS file containing B cell data for pseudotime trajectory analysis.

input_params.name: base_dir
input_params.description: The base directory where the input data and output files will be stored. This should be a valid path on the file system.

input_params.name: num_dim
input_params.description: The number of dimensions to use for the reduced dimensional space in the trajectory analysis. This parameter determines the complexity and detail of the trajectory plot.

input_params.name: cluster_resolution
input_params.description: A floating point number that determines the granularity of clustering. Higher values result in more, smaller clusters, while lower values produce fewer, larger clusters.

input_params.name: min_gene_cells
input_params.description: The minimum number of cells that a gene must be expressed in to be included in the analysis. This threshold helps filter out genes with low expression across the dataset, ensuring more reliable trajectory inference.

input_params.name: root_celltype
