input_params.description: The type of plot to generate, must be one of the following: umap, violin, ridge, heatmap.

run_qc_filtering.description: Quality control filtering for single-cell data based on gene count/UMI count/mitochondrial ratio, output filtered objects

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: The file path to the input RDS (R data) file containing the single-cell data to be filtered.

input_params.name: min_genes
input_params.description: The minimum number of genes that a cell must express to be included in the filtered output. Cells with fewer expressed genes will be excluded.

input_params.name: max_genes
input_params.description: The maximum number of genes a cell can express to be included in the filtered output. Cells expressing more genes than this value will be excluded.

input_params.name: min_counts
input_params.description: The minimum number of counts (UMIs) a cell must have to be retained in the filtered output. Cells with fewer counts than this value will be excluded.

input_params.name: mt_percent
input_params.description: The maximum percentage of mitochondrial gene expression allowed in a cell. Cells with a higher percentage will be filtered out.

run_normalization_sct.description: Normalize single-cell data using SCTransform, output normalized Seurat objects

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: Path to the input Seurat RDS file containing single-cell data to be normalized.

input_params.name: vars_to_regress
input_params.description: A list of variables to regress out during the normalization process. These can include batch effects, cell cycle phase, or any other confounding factors.

input_params.name: n_variable_features
input_params.description: The number of variable features to identify and use for normalization. This parameter specifies how many of the most variable genes should be selected for further analysis.

run_integration_harmony.description: Remove batch effects using Harmony, output integrated Seurat objects and quality control plots

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: Path to the input Seurat RDS file containing the single-cell RNA-seq data to be integrated.

input_params.name: batch_variable
input_params.description: The name of the variable in the Seurat object that defines the batch for each cell. This is used to identify and correct for batch effects.

input_params.name: dims
input_params.description: The number of dimensions to use for the Harmony integration. This parameter specifies how many principal components should be used in the batch effect correction process.

input_params.name: theta
input_params.description: A list of theta values for the Harmony algorithm, which controls the contribution of each batch to the overall correction. Higher values give more weight to the batch-specific correction.

run_clustering_analysis.description: Run graph clustering analysis, output clustering results and UMAP visualization

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing the single-cell RNA sequencing data for clustering analysis.

input_params.name: resolution
input_params.description: A floating point number that controls the granularity of the clustering. Higher values lead to more clusters, while lower values result in fewer, larger clusters.

input_params.name: algorithm
input_params.description: The clustering algorithm to be used for the analysis. Options are limited to 'leiden' or 'louvain'.

input_params.name: dims
input_params.description: The number of dimensions to use for the UMAP visualization. This should be a positive integer.

run_doublet_detection.description: Identify doublets using DoubletFinder, output filtered Seurat objects and quality control plots

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: The file path to the input Seurat object in RDS format that will be used for doublet detection.

input_params.name: expected_doublet_rate
input_params.description: The anticipated proportion of doublets in the dataset, expressed as a decimal. This value is used to set the threshold for doublet detection.

input_params.name: pN
input_params.description: The probability that a cell is a doublet. This value is used to estimate the expected number of doublets in the dataset.

input_params.name: pK
input_params.description: The estimated proportion of doublets in the dataset, used to set the threshold for doublet detection. This value should be a float between 0 and 1.

input_params.name: dims
input_params.description: The number of principal components to use for doublet detection. This parameter specifies the dimensions of the reduced space in which doublets are identified.

run_deg_analysis.description: Run differential gene analysis, support multiple testing methods, output volcano plots and heatmaps

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: The file path to the input RDS file containing the single-cell RNA sequencing data for differential gene analysis.

input_params.name: group_by
input_params.description: The column name in the metadata that defines the groups for differential gene analysis.

input_params.name: ident_1
input_params.description: The identifier for the first group or condition to be compared in the differential gene analysis. This can be a sample name, cell type, or any other relevant label.

input_params.name: ident_2
input_params.description: The identifier for the second group in the comparison. This is used to specify the sample or condition that will be compared against the first group (ident_1) in the differential gene analysis.

input_params.name: test_use
input_params.description: Specifies the statistical test method to be used for differential gene analysis. Options are limited to wilcox, t, MAST, or DESeq2.

input_params.name: logfc_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum log2 fold change value to consider a gene as differentially expressed. This threshold helps in filtering genes based on the magnitude of their expression change.

input_params.name: min_pct
input_params.description: The minimum percentage of cells in which a gene must be detected to be included in the analysis. This value should be a float between 0 and 1.

run_marker_detection.description: Identify marker genes for all clusters, output marker gene tables and visualization plots

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: The file path to the input RDS file containing the single-cell RNA sequencing data.

input_params.name: group_by
input_params.description: The column name in the metadata that defines the groups or clusters for which marker genes will be identified.

input_params.name: only_pos
input_params.description: A boolean flag to indicate whether to consider only positive markers (True) or both positive and negative markers (False).

input_params.name: min_pct
input_params.description: The minimum percentage of cells in a cluster that must express a gene for it to be considered as a marker. This value should be between 0 and 1.

input_params.name: logfc_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum log2 fold change threshold for a gene to be considered a marker. Genes with a log2 fold change below this value will not be included in the marker list.

input_params.name: top_n
input_params.description: The number of top marker genes to identify for each cluster. This integer value determines how many of the most significant marker genes will be selected and reported for each cluster.

run_pathway_enrichment.description: Run pathway enrichment analysis (GO/KEGG/GSEA), output enrichment tables and dot plots

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: The file path to the input RDS file containing the single-cell RNA sequencing data for pathway enrichment analysis.

input_params.name: deg_csv
input_params.description: Path to the CSV file containing differentially expressed genes (DEGs) for pathway enrichment analysis.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The organism for which the pathway enrichment analysis is to be performed. Options are limited to 'human' or 'mouse'.

input_params.name: ontology
input_params.description: The biological aspect to focus on for the enrichment analysis, constrained to options: BP (Biological Process), MF (Molecular Function), CC (Cellular Component).

input_params.name: pvalue_cutoff
input_params.description: The threshold for the p-value used to determine statistical significance in the enrichment analysis. Lower values indicate a more stringent cutoff.

input_params.name: qvalue_cutoff
input_params.description: The threshold for the q-value used to filter significant pathways. Pathways with a q-value below this cutoff will be considered significant.

run_dim_reduction.description: Run dimensionality reduction analysis (PCA/UMAP/tSNE), output embedding results and visualization plots

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: The path to the input RDS file containing the single-cell RNA sequencing data for dimensionality reduction analysis.

input_params.name: methods
input_params.description: A list of dimensionality reduction methods to be applied. Options include 'PCA', 'UMAP', and 'tSNE'.

input_params.name: dims
input_params.description: The number of dimensions to reduce the data to for the analysis. This should be an integer value.

input_params.name: n_neighbors
input_params.description: The number of nearest neighbors to consider for each point in the dataset, which influences the local structure preservation in the reduced dimension space.

input_params.name: min_dist
input_params.description: The minimum distance between points in the embedding, controlling the level of clustering. A smaller value will result in a more clustered layout.

run_subset_cells.description: Filter cell subsets by metadata, output subset Seurat objects and statistics

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: The file path to the input Seurat RDS object that contains the single-cell RNA data to be subsetted.

input_params.name: subset_column
input_params.description: The name of the column in the metadata that will be used to filter the cell subsets. This column should contain categorical data that defines the groups or categories for subsetting.

input_params.name: subset_values
input_params.description: A list of strings specifying the values to use for subsetting cells based on metadata. Each string should correspond to a value in the metadata column used for filtering.

input_params.name: invert
input_params.description: A boolean value that, if true, inverts the selection criteria to include cells that do not match the specified metadata filters.

run_full_preprocessing_pipeline.description: Complete single-cell data preprocessing pipeline (QC→normalization→dimensionality reduction→clustering), output final RDS

input_params.name: input_rds
input_params.description: Path to the input RDS file containing the raw single-cell data to be preprocessed.

input_params.name: min_genes
input_params.description: The minimum number of genes a cell must express to be included in the analysis. This parameter is used for quality control to filter out low-quality cells.

input_params.name: max_genes
input_params.description: The maximum number of genes to be considered per cell after filtering. This parameter helps in removing cells with an unusually high number of detected genes, which might indicate poor quality or doublets.

input_params.name: min_counts
input_params.description: The minimum number of counts a cell must have to be included in the analysis. Cells with fewer counts than this value will be filtered out.

input_params.name: mt_percent
input_params.description: The percentage of mitochondrial gene expression in each cell. This value is used to filter out low-quality cells with high mitochondrial content, which often indicates cellular stress or apoptosis.

input_params.name: n_variable_features
input_params.description: The number of highly variable features to be selected for further analysis in the preprocessing pipeline.

input_params.name: vars_to_regress
input_params.description: A list of variable names to regress out during the preprocessing. These variables are typically unwanted sources of variation, such as batch effects or cell cycle stages.

input_params.name: resolution
input_params.description: A floating-point number that controls the granularity of the clustering. Higher values lead to more clusters, while lower values result in fewer, coarser clusters.

input_params.name: dims
input_params.description: The number of dimensions to retain after dimensionality reduction.

download_sabdab_summary_csv.description: Download SAbDab summary CSV containing antibody PDB, chain information, and other metadata

input_params.name: filters
input_params.description: A dictionary where each key-value pair represents a filter to be applied to the SAbDab summary data. The key is the column name and the value is the condition that must be met for that column.

input_params.name: save_file
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether the downloaded SAbDab summary CSV should be saved to a file. If true, the file will be saved; if false, it will not be saved.

download_pdb_structure.description: Download numbered antibody PDB structures from SAbDab, output file contents and paths

input_params.name: pdb_id
input_params.description: The unique identifier for the Protein Data Bank (PDB) entry to be downloaded.

input_params.name: numbering_scheme
input_params.description: The numbering scheme to be used for the antibody PDB structures. Must be one of the following: chothia, kabat, imgt.

input_params.name: save_file
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether the downloaded PDB structure should be saved to a file. If true, the file will be saved; if false, it will not.

download_sabdab_dataset.description: Download complete SAbDab dataset, return data content and metadata

input_params.name: dataset_type
input_params.description: Specifies the type of dataset to download. Options are constrained to: all, antigen_bound, nanobodies, fabs, scfvs.

input_params.name: output_format
input_params.description: The desired format for the downloaded SAbDab dataset. Options are limited to csv, json, or fasta.

input_params.name: save_file
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether the downloaded dataset should be saved as a file or not. If true, the dataset will be saved to a file; if false, it will not be saved and only returned in the response.

get_sabdab_statistics.description: Get SAbDab database statistics and metadata

input_params.name: save_file
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to save the retrieved statistics and metadata to a file.

prepare_bindcraft_config.description: Generate BindCraft design configuration file, return configuration ID and file path

input_params.name: target_pdb
input_params.description: A PDB file that specifies the target protein structure for the BindCraft design process.

input_params.name: binder_name
input_params.description: The name of the binder to be used in the BindCraft design configuration. This should be a string that uniquely identifies the binder.

input_params.name: chains
input_params.description: A string specifying the chains to be included in the design, typically represented by chain IDs or names.

input_params.name: hotspot
input_params.description: A string specifying a particular region of interest in the design, often used to focus the design process on a specific area. If not provided, the entire design is considered.

input_params.name: min_length
input_params.description: The minimum length of the sequence to be considered in the design. It must be a positive integer.

input_params.name: max_length
input_params.description: The maximum length of the sequence to be designed. This parameter specifies the upper limit for the length of the DNA or protein sequence in the design process.

input_params.name: num_designs
input_params.description: The number of design configurations to generate. This should be a positive integer.

input_params.name: design_algorithm
input_params.description: The algorithm to be used for the design process. Options are constrained to '2stage', '3stage', '4stage', 'greedy', or 'mcmc'.

input_params.name: design_path
input_params.description: The file path to the design input file that will be used for generating the BindCraft configuration. If not provided, a default path may be used.

validate_target_pdb.description: Validate target PDB structure integrity, estimate GPU memory requirements, provide design suggestions

input_params.name: pdb_file
input_params.description: A PDB file containing the 3D structure of the target protein to be validated.

run_bindcraft_design.description: Run BindCraft binder design pipeline, support async/sync modes, return task ID

input_params.name: settings_path
input_params.description: Path to a JSON file containing the settings for the BindCraft design pipeline, including parameters such as sequence, structure constraints, and optimization criteria.

input_params.name: filters_path
input_params.description: A JSON file containing the filtering criteria for the binder design, such as sequence length, binding affinity, and other molecular properties.

input_params.name: advanced_path
input_params.description: A JSON file containing advanced configuration settings for the BindCraft design pipeline, such as specific algorithm parameters or custom constraints.

input_params.name: async_mode
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether the BindCraft design pipeline should run in asynchronous mode. If true, the task will be processed in the background and a task ID will be returned immediately. If false, the tool will wait for the process to complete and return the results directly.

check_design_status.description: Monitor BindCraft design task progress, return trajectory count, sequence count, running status, and resource usage

input_params.name: job_id
input_params.description: A unique identifier for the BindCraft design task to be monitored.

analyze_design_results.description: Analyze BindCraft design results, sort by comprehensive/single metrics, output sequences, metrics, statistics, and PDB paths

input_params.name: design_path
input_params.description: The file path to the BindCraft design results that need to be analyzed.

input_params.name: top_n
input_params.description: The number of top results to be included in the analysis, based on the specified metrics.

input_params.name: sort_by
input_params.description: The metric to sort the design results by. Options are 'composite', 'plddt', 'iptm', 'dg', or 'shape_comp'.

generate_design_report.description: Generate comprehensive BindCraft design report including sequences, structures, metrics, output archive files

input_params.name: design_path
input_params.description: The file path to the design for which the report is to be generated. This should be a valid path to a BindCraft design file.

input_params.name: top_n
input_params.description: The number of top designs to include in the report. This integer value specifies how many of the best design results, based on the evaluation metrics, will be detailed in the generated report.

input_params.name: include_structures
input_params.description: Indicates whether to include structural information in the design report. If true, the report will contain detailed structure data; if false, it will not.

easy_search.description: Fast protein structure search, 10,000x faster than TMalign, support GPU acceleration, for homology identification

input_params.name: query
input_params.description: A PDB file containing the query protein structure to be used for searching against a database of known structures.

input_params.name: target
input_params.description: The target parameter specifies the protein structure database or file against which the query will be searched. This can be a path to a local file or a pre-defined database name if using a supported service.

input_params.name: sensitivity
input_params.description: A value between 0 and 1 that controls the trade-off between search speed and sensitivity. Higher values increase the chance of finding more distant homologs but decrease the speed.

input_params.name: e_value
input_params.description: The e_value parameter specifies the statistical expectation value, or E-value, which is a measure of the number of alignments with the given score expected to occur by chance. Lower values indicate higher significance.

input_params.name: alignment_type
input_params.description: Specifies the type of alignment to be used. Options are constrained to 0, 1, or 2, where 0 is for global alignment, 1 is for local alignment, and 2 is for a custom alignment type.

input_params.name: max_seqs
input_params.description: The maximum number of sequences to be returned from the search. There are no specific options; any positive integer is acceptable.

input_params.name: coverage
input_params.description: The minimum percentage of the query sequence that must be aligned to a target for it to be considered a valid match. This value is a float between 0 and 1.

input_params.name: use_gpu
input_params.description: A boolean value that, when set to true, enables the use of GPU acceleration for faster processing. If false, the tool will run on the CPU.

input_params.name: output_format
input_params.description: The format in which the search results will be output. This can be any string, but typically it should be a recognized format such as 'tsv', 'csv', or 'json'.

search_from_sequence.description: Quickly search structure databases from amino acid sequences based on ProstT5 3Di encoding, completes in minutes

input_params.name: fasta_input
input_params.description: A file in FASTA format (.fasta or .fa) containing the amino acid sequences to be used for searching the structure databases.

input_params.name: target_database
input_params.description: The name or path of the target structure database to search against. This should be a valid Foldseek or MMseqs2 database.

input_params.name: sensitivity
input_params.description: A value between 0 and 1 that controls the trade-off between search speed and accuracy. A higher value increases sensitivity, potentially at the cost of longer search times.

input_params.name: e_value
input_params.description: The E-value threshold for the search, representing the expected number of hits by chance. Lower values increase the stringency of the search.

input_params.name: use_gpu
input_params.description: Indicates whether to utilize GPU for accelerated processing. True to enable, False to disable.

create_custom_database.description: Create custom Foldseek database from PDB/mmCIF files for structure search

input_params.name: structure_directory
input_params.description: The directory path containing PDB or mmCIF files to be used for creating the Foldseek database.

input_params.name: database_name
input_params.description: The name to assign to the custom Foldseek database, which will be used for referencing in subsequent searches and operations.

cluster_structures.description: Cluster protein structures by structural similarity for deduplication, representative structure selection, and diversity analysis

input_params.name: input_database
input_params.description: The path to the input database file containing protein structures to be clustered. This should be a valid Foldseek or MMseqs2 database file.

input_params.name: cluster_mode
input_params.description: Specifies the clustering mode: 0 for single-linkage, 1 for complete-linkage, and 2 for average-linkage. The value must be one of the following: 0, 1, or 2.

input_params.name: min_seq_id
input_params.description: The minimum sequence identity threshold for clustering, as a float between 0 and 1. Clusters will only be formed if the sequence identity is at or above this value.

input_params.name: coverage
input_params.description: The minimum fraction of the query and target sequence that must be aligned to each other. It is a value between 0 and 1, where 1 means the entire sequences must align.

download_prebuilt_database.description: Download Foldseek pre-built databases (PDB/AlphaFoldDB, etc.), support background operation

input_params.name: database_name
input_params.description: The name of the pre-built database to download. Must be one of 'PDB', 'AlphaFoldDB', 'ESMAtlas', or 'ProstT5'.

check_mpnn_design_novelty.description: Evaluate structural novelty of MPNN-designed sequences using ProstT5+Foldseek, output novelty classification and validation suggestions

input_params.name: sequences
input_params.description: A list of amino acid sequences designed by the MPNN model to be evaluated for structural novelty.

input_params.name: reference_database
input_params.description: The path or identifier of the reference database against which the MPNN-designed sequences will be compared for structural novelty.

input_params.name: novelty_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum similarity score required to classify a sequence as novel. Sequences with a similarity score below this threshold are considered structurally novel.

easy_multimersearch.description: Search protein complex structures, suitable for antibody-antigen complexes, output similarity metrics

input_params.name: query_complex
input_params.description: A string representing the file path or identifier of the query protein complex structure to be searched against the database.

input_params.name: target
input_params.description: The target parameter specifies the protein complex structure or sequence against which the query will be searched. This can be a file path, a database identifier, or a raw sequence.

input_params.name: sensitivity
input_params.description: A value between 0 and 1 that controls the trade-off between search speed and sensitivity. Higher values increase sensitivity but decrease speed.

input_params.name: e_value
input_params.description: The e-value threshold for the search, representing the expected number of hits with a given score or better that would be found by chance. Lower values indicate higher confidence in the match.

input_params.name: alignment_type
input_params.description: Specifies the type of alignment to be used in the search. Options are constrained to 0, 1, or 2, where each number represents a different alignment method.

input_params.name: max_seqs
input_params.description: The maximum number of sequences to include in the output, indicating how many top matches to return from the search.

input_params.name: use_gpu
input_params.description: A boolean flag to indicate whether to utilize GPU for accelerated computation. True to enable, False to disable.

input_params.name: output_format
input_params.description: The format in which the search results will be output. Since no specific options are provided, any valid string that defines a recognized output format by the tool can be used.

easy_multimercluster.description: Cluster protein complex structures considering overall/single-chain/interface similarity, output clustering results and representative sequences

input_params.name: input_complexes
input_params.description: A string specifying the file path to the input protein complex structures that will be used for clustering. The file should contain the necessary structural information in a format compatible with the foldseek service.

input_params.name: multimer_tm_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum TM-score threshold for considering two multimer structures as similar. This value ranges from 0 to 1, where a higher value indicates a stricter similarity requirement.

input_params.name: chain_tm_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum TM-score threshold for considering two chains as similar. The value should be between 0 and 1, where a higher value indicates a stricter similarity requirement.

input_params.name: interface_lddt_threshold
input_params.description: The minimum Local Distance Difference Test (lDDT) score for interfaces to be considered similar. This threshold is used to filter out dissimilar interfaces during the clustering process.

input_params.name: coverage
input_params.description: The minimum percentage of the sequence length that must be aligned for a match to be considered. This value is a float between 0 and 1, where 1 represents 100% coverage.

input_params.name: coverage_mode
input_params.description: Determines the coverage calculation mode for sequence alignment: 0 for global, 1 for local, or 2 for glocal (global-local) alignment.

input_params.name: alignment_type
input_params.description: Specifies the type of alignment to be used for clustering. Options are constrained to 0, 1, or 2, where 0 represents global alignment, 1 represents local alignment, and 2 represents a hybrid approach.

download_mcpas_database.description: Download McPAS-TCR database containing pathology-related TCR sequences, support caching

input_params.name: force_refresh
input_params.description: A boolean flag that, when set to true, forces the tool to refresh and download the McPAS-TCR database, ignoring any cached version. If false or not provided, the tool will use an existing cache if available.

input_params.name: save_path
input_params.description: The file path where the downloaded McPAS-TCR database will be saved. If not provided, a default location may be used.

input_params.name: include_metadata
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to include additional metadata along with the TCR sequences in the downloaded database.

search_mcpas_tcr.description: Search McPAS-TCR database, support filtering and sequence fuzzy matching

input_params.name: cdr3_beta
input_params.description: The CDR3 beta sequence or a list of CDR3 beta sequences to search for in the McPAS-TCR database. This can be used for exact or fuzzy matching depending on other parameters.

input_params.name: cdr3_alpha
input_params.description: The CDR3 alpha sequence or a list of CDR3 alpha sequences to search for in the McPAS-TCR database. This can include exact sequences or sequences with wildcards for fuzzy matching.

input_params.name: epitope
input_params.description: The epitope sequence to search for in the McPAS-TCR database. This can be a specific amino acid sequence of interest.

input_params.name: levenshtein_distance
input_params.description: The maximum number of allowed single-character edits (insertions, deletions, or substitutions) to match the TCR sequence with sequences in the database.

input_params.name: category
input_params.description: The category parameter specifies the type of disease or condition associated with the TCR data, constrained to one of the following options: Pathogens, Cancer, Autoimmune, Other.

input_params.name: pathology
input_params.description: The specific disease or pathological condition to filter the TCR sequences by. If not provided, no pathology-based filtering is applied.

input_params.name: antigen_protein
input_params.description: The name of the antigen protein to filter the search results. This parameter is optional and can be any string value.

input_params.name: mhc
input_params.description: The MHC (Major Histocompatibility Complex) molecule type associated with the TCR. This parameter is optional and can be used to filter results based on a specific MHC class or allele.

input_params.name: species
input_params.description: The species to which the TCR sequences belong. Options are limited to 'human' or 'mouse'.

input_params.name: tissue
input_params.description: The specific tissue type from which the TCR data is derived. If not provided, the search will not be filtered by tissue.

input_params.name: t_cell_type
input_params.description: The type of T cell to filter the search results. Options are limited to CD4, CD8, Treg, Teff, or TIL.

input_params.name: min_quality
input_params.description: Specifies the minimum quality of the TCR data to be included in the search, constrained to options: tetramers, stimulation, all.

input_params.name: ngs_only
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to include only NGS (Next Generation Sequencing) data in the search results.

input_params.name: max_results
input_params.description: The maximum number of results to return from the McPAS-TCR database search.

input_params.name: return_top_n
input_params.description: The number of top results to return from the McPAS-TCR database search.

input_params.name: output_format
input_params.description: The format in which the search results will be returned. Options are limited to json, csv, or tsv.

get_mcpas_statistics.description: Get McPAS-TCR database statistics including classifications, species, and MHC distribution

input_params.name: statistics_type
input_params.description: The type of statistics to retrieve, constrained to one of the following options: 'overview' for a general summary, 'detailed' for a comprehensive breakdown, or 'by_field' for statistics categorized by specific fields.

input_params.name: group_by
input_params.description: The criterion for grouping the statistics, must be one of: category, species, mhc, or quality.

input_params.name: filters
input_params.description: A dictionary where keys are filter criteria and values are the corresponding filter values to narrow down the statistics from the McPAS-TCR database.

mixtcrpred_list_models.description: List available MixTCRpred models including epitope, MHC, training sample count, and other information

input_params.name: min_training_samples
input_params.description: The minimum number of training samples a model must have to be included in the list.

input_params.name: epitope_filter
input_params.description: A string to filter the list of models by epitope. If provided, only models associated with this epitope will be listed.

input_params.name: mhc_filter
input_params.description: A string to filter the MHC types. If provided, only models with MHC types matching this string will be listed.

input_params.name: only_downloaded
input_params.description: A boolean flag to indicate whether to list only the models that have been downloaded locally. If set to true, only the downloaded models will be listed; if false, all available models (both downloaded and not downloaded) will be listed.

mixtcrpred_download_models.description: Download MixTCRpred models from Zenodo, support specified/all/high-confidence models

input_params.name: model_names
input_params.description: A list of strings specifying the names of the MixTCRpred models to be downloaded. If left empty, all available models will be downloaded.

input_params.name: download_mode
input_params.description: Specifies the mode of download: 'specific' for user-specified models, 'all' to download all available models, or 'high_confidence' to download only high-confidence models.

mixtcrpred_validate_input.description: Validate TCR input data format, check sequence validity and required columns

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: The path to the TCR input data file to be validated. This file should contain the TCR sequences and other required columns for analysis.

mixtcrpred_predict.description: Predict TCR-epitope binding using MixTCRpred, output binding scores and percentile rankings

input_params.name: model_name
input_params.description: The name of the pre-trained model to be used for TCR-epitope binding prediction. This should be a string that identifies a specific model available in the MixTCRpred library.

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: Path to the input file containing TCR-epitope sequences for binding prediction.

input_params.name: output_file
input_params.description: The filename for the output file where the binding scores and percentile rankings will be saved.

input_params.name: batch_size
input_params.description: The number of samples to process in one batch during the prediction. This parameter controls the trade-off between memory usage and speed.

input_params.name: host_species
input_params.description: The host species for which the TCR-epitope binding is to be predicted. Must be one of: 'HomoSapiens', 'MusMusculus'.

design_sequences_from_backbone.description: Design sequences based on protein backbone for antibody optimization, stability improvement, and de novo design

input_params.name: pdb_path
input_params.description: The file path to the PDB file containing the protein backbone structure.

input_params.name: chains
input_params.description: A list of chain identifiers to be considered for sequence design. Each element in the list should be a string representing a single chain identifier.

input_params.name: num_sequences
input_params.description: The number of sequences to generate based on the provided protein backbone. This integer value determines how many unique sequence designs will be produced.

input_params.name: temperature
input_params.description: A value that controls the randomness of the sequence design. Higher values lead to more diverse and potentially less optimal designs, while lower values produce more conservative and likely higher quality designs.

input_params.name: omit_amino_acids
input_params.description: A list of amino acids to be excluded from the sequence design. Each element in the list should be a single-letter code for an amino acid.

input_params.name: seed
input_params.description: An integer used to initialize the random number generator for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed will be used.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the output files will be saved. If not provided, a default directory may be used.

score_sequences_on_backbone.description: Evaluate sequence compatibility with protein backbone for mutation validation and library screening

input_params.name: pdb_path
input_params.description: The file path to the PDB file containing the protein backbone structure for sequence evaluation.

input_params.name: sequences
input_params.description: A list of amino acid sequences to be evaluated for compatibility with the protein backbone. Each sequence should be a string of single-letter amino acid codes.

input_params.name: chain_id
input_params.description: The identifier for the specific chain within the protein structure to be analyzed. If not provided, the tool will consider all chains.

input_params.name: per_residue
input_params.description: Indicates whether to return scores for each individual residue in the sequence, rather than a single score for the entire sequence.

design_with_fixed_positions.description: Design sequences with fixed key positions, preserve CDR regions, functional residues, and disulfide bond cysteines

input_params.name: pdb_path
input_params.description: The file path to the PDB file that contains the structure for which sequences will be designed.

input_params.name: fixed_positions
input_params.description: A dictionary where keys are chain identifiers and values are lists of residue positions that should remain fixed during the design process.

input_params.name: design_positions
input_params.description: A dictionary specifying the positions in the sequence that are to be designed, with each key being a chain identifier and the value a list of integer position indices on that chain.

input_params.name: num_sequences
input_params.description: The number of sequences to generate. This should be a positive integer indicating how many different sequence designs are desired.

input_params.name: temperature
input_params.description: A float value that controls the randomness of the sequence design. Higher values lead to more diverse and potentially less stable designs, while lower values produce more conservative and stable designs.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory where the output files will be saved. If not provided, the default output directory is used.

design_with_amino_acid_bias.description: Sequence design with physicochemical constraints, avoid sensitive residues, control hydrophobicity/charge distribution

input_params.name: pdb_path
input_params.description: The file path to the PDB file that contains the structure to be used as a template for sequence design.

input_params.name: chains
input_params.description: A list of chain identifiers for which the sequence design will be applied. If not provided, the design will be applied to all chains.

input_params.name: global_aa_bias
input_params.description: A dictionary specifying the global bias for each amino acid, where keys are single-letter amino acid codes and values are floating point numbers representing the bias. Higher values indicate a stronger preference for the corresponding amino acid in the designed sequence.

input_params.name: per_position_bias
input_params.description: A dictionary specifying the bias for each position in the sequence. The keys are the positions (as strings), and the values are dictionaries where the keys are amino acid one-letter codes, and the values are the corresponding bias scores (floats).

input_params.name: num_sequences
input_params.description: The number of sequences to generate, indicating how many different designs the tool should produce.

input_params.name: temperature
input_params.description: A float value that controls the randomness of the sequence design. Higher values lead to more diverse but potentially less optimal designs, while lower values produce more conservative and optimized designs.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory where the output files will be saved. If not provided, the default output directory is used.

get_position_probabilities.description: Get amino acid probability distribution at each position of sequences for guiding mutation experiments and sequence logo generation

input_params.name: pdb_path
input_params.description: The file path to the Protein Data Bank (PDB) file that contains the 3D structure of the protein of interest.

input_params.name: chain_id
input_params.description: The identifier for the chain in the protein structure. This is used to specify which chain's position probabilities are to be calculated.

input_params.name: reference_sequence
input_params.description: The amino acid sequence to be used as a reference for calculating position probabilities. If not provided, the tool will use a default or generate one based on the input data.

input_params.name: output_format
input_params.description: The format in which the output data should be returned, with options limited to json or csv.

design_with_pssm.description: Design sequences based on PSSM evolutionary information, balance structural and evolutionary constraints

input_params.name: pdb_path
input_params.description: The file path to the PDB structure that will be used as the template for sequence design.

input_params.name: pssm_file
input_params.description: A file containing the Position-Specific Scoring Matrix (PSSM) that provides evolutionary information to guide the sequence design process.

input_params.name: pssm_weight
input_params.description: A floating point number that determines the weight of the PSSM (Position-Specific Scoring Matrix) in the sequence design process, balancing it against structural constraints.

input_params.name: chains
input_params.description: A list of chain identifiers for which the sequences will be designed. Each identifier should correspond to a specific chain in the protein structure.

input_params.name: num_sequences
input_params.description: The number of sequences to generate, indicating how many distinct sequence designs the tool should produce.

input_params.name: temperature
input_params.description: A float value that controls the randomness of the sequence design. Higher values lead to more diverse but potentially less stable designs, while lower values produce more conservative and stable designs.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory where the output files will be saved. If not specified, the default output directory is used.

batch_design_and_score.description: Batch process PDB structures for antibody library design, mutation optimization, and large-scale screening

input_params.name: pdb_directory
input_params.description: The directory path containing PDB files to be processed for antibody library design, mutation optimization, and large-scale screening.

input_params.name: num_sequences_per_structure
input_params.description: The number of sequences to generate for each input PDB structure, used for creating a diverse library or exploring sequence space.

input_params.name: temperature
input_params.description: The temperature value used in the mutation optimization process, controlling the randomness of the mutations. Higher values lead to more random mutations.

input_params.name: also_score
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to also score the designed or optimized structures in addition to generating them. If true, the tool will provide a scoring for each structure; if false, only the design or optimization will be performed without scoring.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the output files will be saved. If not provided, a default directory may be used.

scan_protein_for_epitopes.description: Scan protein sequences to predict potential T cell epitopes, output binding affinity and rankings

input_params.name: sequence_input
input_params.description: Amino acid sequence of the protein to be scanned for potential T cell epitopes. The sequence should be provided in one-letter code format.

input_params.name: peptide_lengths
input_params.description: The lengths of the peptides to be analyzed for potential T cell epitopes. Must be one of the following values: 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, or 14.

input_params.name: hla_alleles
input_params.description: A list of HLA alleles to be used for predicting T cell epitopes. Each allele should be specified in the standard nomenclature, e.g., 'HLA-A*02:01'.

input_params.name: threshold
input_params.description: The minimum binding affinity threshold for epitopes to be considered. Only epitopes with a binding affinity at or below this value will be reported.

input_params.name: sort_by
input_params.description: Determines the sorting criteria for the predicted epitopes. Must be one of: rank, affinity, or position.

input_params.name: output_format
input_params.description: The format in which the prediction results will be returned. Options are limited to 'json' for a structured JSON output or 'csv' for a comma-separated values file.

predict_peptide_mhc_binding.description: Predict peptide-MHC binding, output binding affinity, rankings, and binding levels

input_params.name: peptides
input_params.description: A list of peptide sequences for which MHC binding affinity will be predicted. Each sequence should be a string of amino acids.

input_params.name: hla_alleles
input_params.description: A list of HLA alleles for which the peptide-MHC binding is to be predicted. Each allele should be specified in the standard HLA nomenclature (e.g., 'HLA-A*02:01').

input_params.name: return_non_binders
input_params.description: Indicates whether to include non-binding peptides in the output. If true, non-binding peptides will be included; if false, only binding peptides are returned.

input_params.name: output_format
input_params.description: The format in which the prediction results will be returned. Options are limited to 'json' or 'csv'.

list_hla_alleles.description: List HLA alleles supported by NetMHCpan, include supertype classifications

input_params.name: species
input_params.description: The species for which to list the HLA alleles. Options are limited to 'human', 'mouse', or 'all'.

input_params.name: locus
input_params.description: The HLA locus to list alleles for, constrained to the options: all, A, B, C, E.

input_params.name: supertype_representatives_only
input_params.description: If true, only HLA alleles that are supertype representatives are listed. If false, all supported HLA alleles are listed.

check_peptide_support.description: Verify if peptides are supported by pre-trained models, return model availability and suggestions

input_params.name: peptide
input_params.description: Amino acid sequence of the peptide to be checked for model support.

input_params.name: model_type
input_params.description: The type of model to use for verification. Options are 'peptide', 'pretrained', or 'pan'.

validate_tcr_input.description: Validate TCR input data format, check sequence validity and column completeness

input_params.name: input_data
input_params.description: A string containing TCR input data, which should include all necessary columns and valid sequences for TCRs.

input_params.name: check_peptide_support
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to check for the presence and validity of peptide support in the TCR input data.

input_params.name: model_type
input_params.description: The type of model to be used for validation, must be one of 'peptide', 'pretrained', or 'pan'.

predict_tcr_binding_fast.description: NetTCR fast prediction, single model inference, suitable for large-scale initial screening

input_params.name: input_data
input_params.description: A string containing the TCR and peptide sequences to be used for binding prediction. The format should be 'TRAV12-3*01/TRAJ18*01_CASRGDTYEQYF/FLRGRAYG'.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the output files will be saved. This should be a valid, writable directory on the system.

input_params.name: model_type
input_params.description: The type of model to use for prediction. Options are 'peptide' for models trained on specific peptides, 'pretrained' for general TCR-antigen binding, and 'pan' for pan-specific TCR predictions.

input_params.name: model_name
input_params.description: The name of the model to be used for TCR binding prediction. This should be a string that identifies a specific, pre-trained model available in the NetTCR fast prediction service.

input_params.name: use_pan_fallback
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to use a pan-specific model as a fallback when the specific model is not available.

predict_tcr_binding_ensemble.description: NetTCR ensemble model prediction, average multiple model results, balance speed and accuracy

input_params.name: input_data
input_params.description: A string containing the TCR sequence data to be used as input for the NetTCR ensemble model prediction. The format should follow the standard representation of TCR sequences, including both alpha and beta chains if applicable.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the output files will be saved. This should be a valid path accessible to the system running the tool.

input_params.name: model_type
input_params.description: The type of model to use for prediction, either 'peptide' for a model trained on specific peptides or 'pretrained' for a generally trained model.

input_params.name: num_models
input_params.description: The number of models to use in the ensemble for making predictions. A higher number increases accuracy but also computation time.

input_params.name: use_pan_fallback
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to use a pan-specificity fallback model when the primary model fails to provide a prediction.

predict_tcr_binding_complete.description: Complete NetTCR prediction pipeline integrating ensemble models, TCRbase, and statistical rankings, output publication-quality results

input_params.name: input_data
input_params.description: The input data for the NetTCR prediction, typically a string containing TCR sequences and related information in a specified format.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the output files will be saved. This should be a valid path accessible to the system running the tool.

input_params.name: use_tcrbase
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to utilize TCRbase for enhanced prediction accuracy and additional context in the results.

input_params.name: tcrbase_alpha
input_params.description: An integer representing the TCRbase alpha chain identifier used in the prediction pipeline.

input_params.name: calculate_percentile
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to calculate the percentile ranking of the predicted binding affinity. If true, the percentile is calculated; if false, it is not.

input_params.name: show_similarity_analysis
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to include a similarity analysis in the output, enhancing the interpretability of the results.

input_params.name: percentile_threshold
input_params.description: The percentile threshold used to filter the prediction results, indicating the top percentage of predictions to be considered. This value should be an integer between 0 and 100.

input_params.name: model_type
input_params.description: Specifies the type of model to be used for prediction. Options are 'pretrained' for using pre-trained models or 'peptide' for peptide-specific models.

list_available_peptides.description: List peptides supported by pre-trained models, include HLA associations and training data statistics

input_params.name: model_type
input_params.description: The type of model to list peptides for, must be either 'peptide' or 'pretrained'.

input_params.name: include_stats
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to include training data statistics for each peptide in the output.

tcrdist3_analyze_repertoire.description: Analyze TCR repertoires using tcrdist3, calculate pairwise distance matrices

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: A CSV file containing TCR repertoire data to be analyzed, including columns for TCR sequences and any associated metadata.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The organism from which the TCR repertoire is derived. Must be either 'mouse' or 'human'.

input_params.name: chains
input_params.description: A list specifying the TCR chains to be analyzed. Valid options are 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma', and 'delta'.

input_params.name: compute_distances
input_params.description: A boolean flag to indicate whether to compute pairwise distances between TCR sequences in the repertoire.

input_params.name: cpus
input_params.description: The number of CPU cores to use for parallel processing. This parameter allows the user to specify how many cores should be utilized to speed up the computation of pairwise distance matrices.

tcrdist3_compute_sparse_distances.description: Calculate sparse distance matrices for large-scale TCR repertoires, save memory

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: A CSV file containing the TCR repertoire data to be used for computing sparse distance matrices. The file should include columns with TCR sequences and any additional metadata required for the analysis.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The organism for which the TCR repertoire is being analyzed. Must be one of 'mouse' or 'human'.

input_params.name: chains
input_params.description: A list specifying the TCR chains to be considered for distance calculation. Valid options are 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma', and 'delta'.

input_params.name: radius
input_params.description: The maximum distance threshold for considering two TCR sequences as neighbors. Any pair of sequences with a distance greater than this value will not be included in the sparse distance matrix.

input_params.name: chunk_size
input_params.description: The number of TCR sequences to process in each chunk, used to manage memory usage during distance calculations.

input_params.name: cpus
input_params.description: The number of CPU cores to use for parallel processing. This parameter allows the user to specify how many cores should be utilized to speed up the computation of sparse distance matrices.

input_params.name: output_file
input_params.description: The file path where the output sparse distance matrix will be saved. This should be a valid file path and name, with an appropriate extension (e.g., .npz for compressed numpy arrays).

tcrdist3_find_neighbors.description: Find TCR neighbors within specified radius for clonotype clustering

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: A CSV file containing TCR sequences and associated data for clonotype clustering.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The organism for which the TCR sequences are derived. Must be either 'mouse' or 'human'.

input_params.name: chain
input_params.description: Specifies the T-cell receptor chain to be used for finding neighbors. Must be one of 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma', or 'delta'.

input_params.name: radius
input_params.description: The maximum distance within which TCR sequences are considered neighbors. It is an integer value representing the radius in the TCR sequence space.

input_params.name: use_sparse
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether to use sparse matrix representation for distance calculations, which can be more memory efficient for large datasets.

tcrdist3_identify_public_tcrs.description: Identify public/quasi-public TCR clonotypes, output cross-individual clonotypes

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: A CSV file containing TCR (T-cell receptor) sequences and associated metadata for analysis.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The organism from which the TCR sequences are derived, must be either 'mouse' or 'human'.

input_params.name: chain
input_params.description: Specifies the T-cell receptor chain to be analyzed. Must be one of 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma', or 'delta'.

input_params.name: radius
input_params.description: The maximum distance between TCR sequences to be considered as part of the same clonotype. This value defines the threshold for clustering similar TCRs.

input_params.name: min_subjects
input_params.description: The minimum number of subjects in which a TCR clonotype must be found to be considered public or quasi-public.

input_params.name: min_neighbors
input_params.description: The minimum number of neighbors a TCR clonotype must have to be considered public or quasi-public. This value determines the threshold for identifying cross-individual clonotypes.

tcrdist3_generate_background.description: Generate background TCR repertoires using OLGA, support V-J frequency matching

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: The path to the input file containing TCR sequences and metadata for generating background repertoires.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The species for which the TCR repertoire is being generated. Must be one of the following: 'human', 'mouse'.

input_params.name: chain
input_params.description: Specifies the T-cell receptor chain to be used for generating the background repertoire. Must be one of 'alpha' or 'beta'.

input_params.name: background_size
input_params.description: The number of TCR sequences to generate for the background repertoire. This value must be a positive integer.

input_params.name: background_type
input_params.description: Specifies the type of background TCR repertoire to generate. Options are 'vj_matched' for V-J frequency matching or 'flat' for a uniform distribution.

input_params.name: output_file
input_params.description: The file path where the generated background TCR repertoire will be saved. This should include the desired filename and extension.

input_params.name: database_path
input_params.description: The file path to the database used for generating background TCR repertoires. This should point to a valid OLGA database.

tcrdist3_calculate_radii.description: Calculate TCR distance radii for FDR-controlled clustering analysis

input_params.name: target_file
input_params.description: The file path to the target TCR sequence data in a supported format, used as input for calculating TCR distance radii.

input_params.name: background_file
input_params.description: The file path to a background TCR repertoire dataset used for calculating the radii. This file should be in a format compatible with tcrdist3, typically a CSV or TSV containing TCR sequences and associated metadata.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The species for which the TCR distance radii are to be calculated. Must be either 'human' or 'mouse'.

input_params.name: chain
input_params.description: The T-cell receptor chain to be used for the distance calculation. Must be one of 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma', or 'delta'.

input_params.name: ctrl_bkgd
input_params.description: A floating point number representing the control background for FDR-controlled clustering analysis.

input_params.name: max_radius
input_params.description: The maximum TCR distance radius to consider for FDR-controlled clustering. This value sets the upper limit for the radii calculation.

input_params.name: cpus
input_params.description: The number of CPU cores to use for parallel processing. There are no specific options; any positive integer is acceptable.

tcrdist3_infer_cdrs.description: Infer CDR1/CDR2 sequences from V genes for complete TCR similarity analysis

input_params.name: input_file
input_params.description: The file path to the input data containing TCR sequences and V gene information for CDR1/CDR2 inference.

input_params.name: organism
input_params.description: The organism from which the TCR sequences are derived. Must be either 'human' or 'mouse'.

input_params.name: chains
input_params.description: A list specifying the TCR chains to be analyzed, with options limited to 'alpha', 'beta', 'gamma', or 'delta'.

input_params.name: imgt_aligned
input_params.description: A boolean flag indicating whether the input sequences are IMGT-aligned. If true, the sequences are expected to be in IMGT alignment format; if false, they are not.

input_params.name: output_file
input_params.description: The file path where the inferred CDR1/CDR2 sequences will be saved. This should be a valid file path and name, with an appropriate file extension (e.g., .txt, .csv).

ribonn_predict.description: Predict mRNA translation efficiency using RiboNN, support human/mouse species, output result files

input_params.name: input_path
input_params.description: The file path to the input data for RiboNN prediction. This should be a valid path to a file containing mRNA sequences in a supported format.

input_params.name: species
input_params.description: The species for which the mRNA translation efficiency is to be predicted. Must be either 'human' or 'mouse'.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the result files will be saved. This should be a valid path accessible to the system.

generate_cds.description: Generate optimized CDS sequences from protein sequences

input_params.name: protein_seq
input_params.description: The amino acid sequence of the protein for which the CDS is to be generated. This should be a valid sequence using the standard one-letter IUPAC amino acid codes.

input_params.name: count
input_params.description: The number of optimized CDS sequences to generate for the given protein sequence.

input_params.name: output_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the generated CDS sequences will be saved. This should be a valid path accessible to the system running the tool.

generate_utr.description: Generate 5'/3' UTR sequences of specified length, output result file paths

predict_stability.description: Predict UTR sequence stability, support 5'/3' UTR, output result file paths

optimize_codon_sequences.description: Batch optimize codons for protein sequences, support multiple input formats, output DNA sequences

optimize_single_sequence.description: Optimize codons for a single protein sequence, support specified species, output DNA sequences

predict_ribosome_loading.description: Predict ribosome loading rate for RNA sequences, support 5UTR/CDS/mRNA types

predict_5utr_mrl.description: Predict ribosome loading rate for 5' UTR sequences, output result file paths

predict_mrna_mrl.description: Predict ribosome loading rate for complete mRNA sequences, output result file paths

extract_seurat_umap_metadata.description: Extract UMAP coordinates and cell metadata from Seurat RDS, output CSV files

input_params.name: rds_file_path
input_params.description: The file path to the Seurat RDS file containing the UMAP coordinates and cell metadata.

input_params.name: output_path
input_params.description: The file path where the output CSV files containing UMAP coordinates and cell metadata will be saved.

input_params.name: focus_genes
input_params.description: A list of gene names to focus on for the analysis. These genes will be used for extracting specific metadata and UMAP coordinates.

integrate_scbcr_bulk_bcr_data.description: Integrate single-cell and bulk BCR sequencing data, add UMAP and cell type annotations

input_params.name: sc_rna_csv_path
input_params.description: Path to the CSV file containing single-cell RNA sequencing data, which will be used for integration with bulk BCR sequencing data.

input_params.name: bulk_raw_data_dir
input_params.description: The directory containing the raw bulk BCR sequencing data files to be integrated with single-cell BCR data.

input_params.name: umap_coordinates_path
input_params.description: Path to a CSV file containing UMAP coordinates for the cells, which will be used for visualization and integration with BCR data.

input_params.name: output_path
input_params.description: The file path where the integrated and annotated BCR sequencing data will be saved.

integrate_binding_neutralization_experim.description: Integrate antibody binding/neutralization experimental data, standardize to binary labels, output merged results

input_params.name: first_experiment_path
input_params.description: The file path to the first antibody binding/neutralization experiment data. This should be a string representing the location of the file, or null if not provided.

input_params.name: second_experiment_path
input_params.description: The file path to the second experiment's data. This is an optional parameter, and if not provided, only the first experiment's data will be processed.

input_params.name: output_path
input_params.description: The file path where the merged and standardized experimental results will be saved. If not provided, the default output path will be used.

integrate_predictions_with_experimental_.description: Integrate machine learning prediction results with experimental data, output comprehensive datasets with features

input_params.name: feature_data_path
input_params.description: The file path to the feature data CSV that will be integrated with the experimental data. Use a .csv file (convert from Excel if needed).

input_params.name: bind_predict_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the machine learning prediction results are stored. This parameter is optional and should point to a valid directory containing the prediction files.

input_params.name: neu_predict_dir
input_params.description: The directory path where the neural network prediction results are stored. This parameter is optional and should point to a valid directory containing the prediction data.

input_params.name: clone_results_path
input_params.description: The file path to the clone results CSV from machine learning predictions. This is an optional parameter, and if provided, it should point to a valid .csv file.

input_params.name: output_path
input_params.description: The file path where the integrated dataset CSV will be saved. If not provided, the default output location is used.

download_url.description: Download any file from URL, automatically identify format, support streaming download for large files

input_params.name: url
input_params.description: The web address from which the file will be downloaded. It should be a valid URL pointing to the file.

input_params.name: output_path

merge_csv_cartesian.description: Cartesian product merge of antibody and antigen CSV files, generate all antibody-antigen pairs, stream progress

input_params.name: antibody_file
input_params.description: A CSV file containing antibody data to be used in the Cartesian product merge with an antigen CSV file.

input_params.name: antigen_file
input_params.description: A CSV file containing the list of antigens to be paired with antibodies. The file should have a column for antigen identifiers.

input_params.name: antibody_key
input_params.description: The column name in the antibody CSV file to use as the key for merging. If not provided, the first column will be used as the key.

input_params.name: antigen_key
input_params.description: The column name in the antigen CSV file to use as the key for the Cartesian product merge. If not provided, the first column will be used as the default key.

input_params.name: new_key_name
input_params.description: The name of the new key that will be created in the merged CSV to represent the Cartesian product of antibody and antigen pairs. If not provided, a default key name will be used.

input_params.name: output_file
input_params.description: The file path where the resulting CSV with all antibody-antigen pairs will be saved. If not provided, the output is not saved to a file.

merge_csv_by_key.description: Merge two CSV files by key, automatically handle column name conflicts, stream progress

input_params.name: input_file1
input_params.description: The first CSV file to be merged, containing the key column for merging with the second CSV file.

input_params.name: input_file2
input_params.description: The second CSV file to be merged, containing at least one common key with the first input file.

input_params.name: key_column
input_params.description: The name of the column to use as the key for merging the two CSV files. If not provided, the first column will be used as the key.

input_params.name: output_file
input_params.description: The path to the output file where the merged CSV will be saved. If not provided, the result will be returned as a string.

create_csv.description: Create blank or header-only CSV files, support temporary files, stream progress

input_params.name: output_file
input_params.description: The path to the output CSV file. If not provided, a temporary file will be created.

input_params.name: columns
input_params.description: A list of strings representing the column names for the CSV file. If provided, a header row will be included in the CSV file.

input_params.name: csv_file
input_params.description: The file path to the CSV file containing BCR data to be integrated.

input_params.name: rds_file
input_params.description: The path to the RDS file containing BCR data to be integrated.

input_params.name: output_file
input_params.description: The name of the file where the integrated BCR data will be saved. It should include the desired file extension, such as .csv or .txt.

input_params.name: csv_fields
input_params.description: A string specifying the fields in the CSV file to be used for integration, separated by commas. If not provided, all fields will be used.

input_params.name: rds_fields
input_params.description: A string specifying the fields from the RDS (Relational Database Service) to be used in the integration process. This can include specific column names or field identifiers relevant for the BCR data integration.

input_params.name: separator
input_params.description: A string that specifies the delimiter used to separate values in the input data. If not provided, the default separator is assumed to be a comma (',').

input_params.name: skip_umap
input_params.description: A boolean value indicating whether to skip the UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) step during data integration. If true, the UMAP step is skipped; if false, it is included.

input_params.name: skip_annotation
input_params.description: A boolean value that, when set to true, indicates that the annotation step should be skipped during the data integration process.
